issue: Profile hash/id semantics changed without a durable migration note for old artifacts keyed by the flat local profile shape.
action: Added a 0.94.0 migration note that calls out canonical AgentProfile, hash/id changes, sourceProfile.kind changes, and no legacy aliases.
verification: CHANGELOG.md contains the 0.94.0 migration section; pnpm build

issue: Marketing demo runs could be accidentally committed through .marketing-agent-runs/.
The resource array order invariant was behavior-bearing but not pinned by a test.
action: Added a hash test proving reordered skills produce different profile hashes.

issue: agentProfileId claimed collision resistance but used only a 48-bit suffix and unsanitized labels in path-like keys.
action: Changed ids to path-safe labels plus a 64-bit behavior-hash suffix.

issue: Top-level merge accepted nested partial options before the dedicated nested merge logic, making the type boundary harder to reason about.
action: Split budget, llm, and hostedTenant overrides before top-level merge.

issue: Zero reps could flow into evaluate defaults or per-call evaluate options and create a zero-cell run instead of failing at the API boundary.
action: Added positive-integer validation for default budget reps, merged improve budget reps, and per-call evaluate reps.

issue: AgentEvalImproveOptions exposed HostedTenant but the /contract public surface did not re-export that type.
action: Re-exported HostedTenant from the contract entry point.

issue: Hosted tenant override test inspected only the last request, so it could miss split eval-run and trace ingestion regressions.
action: Captured all requests and asserted endpoint, auth, tenant id, eval-runs, and traces behavior across the batch.

issue: Review comment suggested nested undefined compaction might be incomplete.
action: No code change: canonicalize/JSON.stringify already drop undefined object properties for the current profile shape; recursive compaction would be broader than the observed issue.

issue: Review comment noted repeated profile hash/id recomputation.
action: No code change: this is cosmetic and not measurable DX/UX risk compared with the API-boundary fixes above.
